title: Writer Integration

deliverables.description: Lesson retrieval script - queries semantic memory for voice preferences by content type
Integration test script - shows combined voice context (lessons + primitives) for Writer
Updated voice transformation system prompt (v3.1) with steps 2b and 4b for lesson retrieval/application
changes: Added step 2b: RETRIEVE voice lessons (from V's past corrections)
Added step 4b: APPLY voice lessons (avoid anti-patterns, use preferred patterns)
Added Voice Lessons to Core Components section
Added section 2b under Key Principles with retrieval command and rationale

deliverables.description: Updated Writer Worker instructions with voice lessons retrieval step

sample_lesson.lesson: Remove all polite hedging and filler phrases that soften the message
sample_lesson.anti_pattern: "Hi [Name]", "wanted to reach out because", "Would you be open to", "Best, [Name]"
sample_lesson.positive_pattern: Start directly with value (no greeting), eliminate hedging verbs, end with confident low-friction question (no sign-off)

empty_handling.behavior: Graceful fallback - shows 'No lessons found for this content type. Proceed with general voice guidance.'
empty_handling.fallback_source: voice-lessons.md file when semantic memory empty

retrieval_params.--content-type: Required. Filter by content type (cold_email, linkedin_post, memo, etc.)
retrieval_params.--list-types: List all content types that have lessons
retrieval_params.--fallback-only: Skip semantic memory, only use voice-lessons.md

notes_for_orchestrator: Lessons are already in semantic memory from W3.1's predecessor work - 6 cold_email lessons found

The retrieve_primitives.py script doesn't have a 'domains' filter that matches 'cold_email' - primitives use different domain tags (career, negotiation, etc.). Consider adding content_type-based primitive retrieval or mapping content_types to primitive domains.
voice-lessons.md exists but is empty (placeholders) - the markdown fallback won't help until that file is populated. Recommend W3.1 also write to this file when storing lessons.
The integration test output format is designed to be copy-pasteable into Writer context. Could be wrapped as a callable function for persona prompts.
Content type detection could be automated - currently relies on explicit --content-type flag. Consider adding auto-detection based on request context.
